purpose: The commit fixes a bug where commands could be queued after device unregistration, leading to use-after-free issues. It adds an error return when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary header include to clean up the code structure.
The commit updates documentation for a new 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c, reflecting changes in the report table.
The commit addresses issues with vma lock handling during split and unmapping. It fixes race conditions and ensures proper allocation of locks to prevent bugs.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD power supply documentation for Realtek rt5682s codec in device tree bindings.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the remove function by adding an iounmap() call where it was missing.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which is part of code cleanup.
The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed to prevent warnings from static checkers when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in RISC-V defconfigs without introducing any functional changes.
The commit replaces an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode with a new function, improving code maintainability and readability.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip when the Real-Time Clock (RTC) hardware is not present, preventing unnecessary test failures.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to variable 'bytes' which was causing a dead store warning. It cleans up the code without affecting functionality.
The commit modifies rcuscale to function without RCU Tasks, reducing unnecessary code inclusion and improving efficiency.
The commit removes a duplicated string 'be split' in the spte.h file, improving code readability.
The commit reverts a previous change that populated the DCN315 clock table based on dcfclk due to issues causing a no read situation when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds device tree bindings documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU by including a compatible string.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for naming consistency within the DW PCIe core driver.
The commit fixes a clang compiler warning related to format specifiers by adjusting the printf-like function argument types for better compatibility and code quality.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label implementation by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long, preventing negative offsets from triggering a warning.
The commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() and calls a function directly to simplify the code, part of an effort to remove an unwanted HAL layer.
The commit changes non-global symbols to static in arm64 CPU errata code to fix sparse warnings.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags in device tree bindings for MDSS nodes, correcting configuration issues.
The commit modifies struct ctrl_ref to allocate space for arrays, preparing for future driver changes in array sizes.
The commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver.
The commit updates the wifi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions) from A to P, improving the handling of different hardware versions.
The commit removes a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks since the core already handles this, simplifying the code.
The commit aims to reduce the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping related cases together, improving code maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for ARM64, specifically for sm6350-lena, to support future I2C functionality.
The commit improves code comments by clarifying a description, fixing a typo, and correcting a coding style issue.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when failing to acquire a hardware semaphore, typically due to unexpected hardware removal. It adds error logging and exits to prevent the loop.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology, improving code maintainability.
The commit removes the IS_SWAPFILE check because it is always defined and thus unnecessary.
The commit adds functionality to handle GTP echo requests by sending appropriate responses, ensuring compliance with the GTP specification.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate for efficiency and adds cpumask_first_zero(). It improves code effectiveness without fixing bugs or adding features.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver because it's no longer supported by firmware, preventing error messages during roaming.
The commit adds a new board configuration to support a specific codec without an amplifier, connecting it to SSP0.
The commit modifies the handling of zerocopy sendmsg to fail when unsupported by the socket, similar to previous fixes for send requests.

The commit modifies the kerneldoc-preamble to test for xeCJK.sty before loading it. This addresses an issue where xelatex would fail with 'xeCJK.sty not found!' on certain distributions like RHEL 9, despite the presence of 'Noto Sans CJK SC' font.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow macros with 'case' statements without reporting errors, improving the script's accuracy.
The commit refactors the simpledrm driver to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. This change improves code architecture and maintainability without altering functionality.
The commit adds a JPEG decoding worker interface to enable parallel execution of three hardware components on MT8195, enhancing functionality.
The commit removes redundant VPRINTK() calls and a function in libata since the information is covered by tracepoints, improving code maintainability.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in display management, optimizing the usage of extra vblank time to potentially reduce power consumption or improve performance.
The commit fixes a type mismatch causing a CFI failure by correcting the function pointer prototype to use the correct enum type instead of uint32_t.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in device tree bindings from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds missing 'additionalProperties: false'. This improves the accuracy of the binding definitions.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the code by modifying how the array is accessed to prevent the warning.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify code in the sun8i-ss crypto driver.
The commit moves fixed file installation and removal code to the filetable section and adjusts function parameters from io_kiocb to ctx.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes, allowing independent control under a shared power domain.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and integrates its contents into another macro to simplify code structure.
